1: If If thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
2: Thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.
3: Go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.
5: And as a bird from the hand of the fowler.
6: Consider her ways, and be wise:
8: And And gathereth her food in the harvest.
9: When wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?
10: A little folding of the hands to sleep:
11: And thy want as an armed man.
15: Suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.
16: Yea, seven are are an abomination unto him:
17: And hands that shed innocent blood,
18: Feet that be swift in running to mischief,
19: And he that soweth discord among brethren.
20: And forsake not the law of thy mother:
21: And And tie them about thy neck.
22: And when when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.
23: And reproofs of instruction are are the way of life:
24: From the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.
25: Neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
26: And the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.
27: And his clothes not be burned?
28: And his feet not be burned?
29: Whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.
30: To satisfy his soul when he is hungry;
31: He shall give all the substance of his house.
32: He that that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.
33: And his reproach shall not be wiped away.
34: Therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
35: Neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.
